response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable polyglot engineer with a strong background in TypeScript/Node.js, high-throughput systems, and software craftsmanship, but he lacks the core technical requirements for this role. Java appears only peripherally in his skills list with no demonstrated depth in JVM internals, Kotlin is absent entirely, and there is no evidence of Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate experience across his entire employment history. His IDE-adjacent work—NI LabWindows C components and MCP code generators—is too distant from enterprise JVM IDE development to bridge this gap.
strengths: Genuine developer-tooling experience: contributed to a C IDE at National Instruments and builds MCP code generators at re:cinq, showing interest in the tooling space
Demonstrated work on large-scale, high-throughput systems (millions of HTTP requests/second at Sociomantic) with attention to performance and reliability
Strong software-craftsmanship practices (TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD) and consistent track record of taking end-to-end ownership of features
concerns: No Kotlin experience anywhere in the resume; Java is listed as a skill but never a primary language in any role
Zero Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate exposure—the three enterprise frameworks explicitly named as the technical focus of the position
IDE internals experience is peripheral: NI LabWindows is a C IDE (not JVM-based), and MCP tooling is infrastructure-level, not language-server or IDE-plugin development
